### Post by Wowcow on 2007-10-08
I have a Spectre X22 Gamer lcd.  The native rez is 1680x1050, which I have achieved.  However, I cannot seem to home in on why I can't get a decent refresh rate.  I cannot go above 51 Hz. It should be at least 70, if not 75.  Here is my xorg.conf.  

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Wed Sep 12 14:29:35 PDT 2007


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/X11R6/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Unknown"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 110.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 150.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
	Modes	   "1680x1050"	
    EndSubSection
EndSection


I hope someone can help me.

### Post by Golyadkin on 2007-10-08
Pretty much all LCD panels run at a maximum of 60Hz, which can't even be changed actually. This cannot be compared to a CRT panel running at 60Hz, which will give you a braintumor. LCD  panels turn individual pixels on and off, whereas a CRT panel redraws the entire screen each time. 

However, running a game at much more frames per second than the refresh rate is pretty pointless. :)

---

### Post by jrlii on 2007-10-08
We've gotten by with movies at 24 frames per second and TV at 30, so I doubt there is a real need to update faster than 60Hz.

Now, on CRTs faster refresh rates were essential because the phosphors fade rapidly between sweeps of the electron beam and text screens on TVs (as in the days of Commodore 64s &c.) were excruciating to use on account of the flicker.

This was somewhat alleviated by adopting the custom of black backgrounds, but most people (that I knew, anyway) bought dedicated monitors as soon as they could afford 'em. . .

---
